prerequisites: A GitLab account with access to the groups and projects you want to sync.
A GitLab API endpoint (api_url). Use the default gitlab.com or your self-managed instance URL.
Credentials (credentials): either a Personal Access Token or an OAuth application.
For OAuth: a registered GitLab OAuth application providing client_id (Application ID) and client_secret (Client Secret).

authMethods.name: Personal Access Token
authMethods.summary: Authenticate with a GitLab personal access token set as credentials.access_token. Create the token under Edit profile > Access > Personal access tokens and grant it the read_api scope, which provides read-only access to the API including all accessible groups and projects.

authMethods.name: OAuth 2.0
authMethods.summary: Authenticate with a GitLab OAuth application using credentials.client_id (Application ID) and credentials.client_secret (Client Secret). The authorization code flow issues credentials.access_token for API requests and credentials.refresh_token to obtain new access tokens when the current one expires.

setupSteps.title: Choose your GitLab API endpoint
setupSteps.body: Set api_url to the GitLab instance you are connecting to. Leave it at the default gitlab.com for GitLab SaaS, or enter your self-managed instance URL.

setupSteps.title: Create a personal access token (token auth)
setupSteps.body: In GitLab, select your avatar in the upper-right corner, then Edit profile. In the left sidebar select Access > Personal access tokens. Enter a token name and expiration date, select the read_api scope, and select Generate token. Use the generated value as credentials.access_token.

setupSteps.title: Or register an OAuth application (OAuth auth)
setupSteps.body: Register a GitLab OAuth application to obtain its Application ID and Client Secret. Provide these as credentials.client_id and credentials.client_secret. The authorization code flow returns credentials.access_token and credentials.refresh_token.

setupSteps.title: Scope what to sync
setupSteps.body: Optionally set groups / groups_list to GitLab group paths (for example airbytehq) and projects / projects_list to project paths (for example airbytehq/airbyte). If both are left blank, the connector retrieves all groups accessible to the token and syncs their projects.

setupSteps.title: Set the replication start date
setupSteps.body: Optionally set start_date to the earliest date to replicate data from, formatted as YYYY-MM-DDT00:00:00Z.

sources.title: GitLab REST API reference - GitLab Docs
